caseStudy.overview: Agility Robotics' Hero Brand Film was the company's first brand film produced at this scale — a cinematic reintroduction of Agility to the world. More than a product showcase, it was a holistic statement of company vision: who Agility is, what they're building, and why it matters. The film also served as the proving ground for a top-to-bottom refreshed visual identity and newly established art direction guidelines.
caseStudy.team: Small core team across design, video production, and leadership
objectives: Reintroduce Agility Robotics to the world with a cinematic, brand-defining film
Showcase the refreshed visual identity and establish a new art direction standard
Communicate a cohesive, forward-looking vision for the company
Create an asset that could serve both external marketing and investor/partner audiences
caseStudy.challenge: The central challenge was presenting a completely refreshed brand for the first time — this film had to carry and prove the new visual identity from frame one. Every design decision was both a creative choice and a precedent-setting one. Communicating a holistic company vision in a way that was emotionally resonant, visually polished, and true to the new brand required tight alignment between design and production throughout.
caseStudy.approach: My role was focused on the visual and brand layer of the film: designing on-screen graphics, contributing to visual brand direction, and providing feedback across edit rounds to ensure the new identity was being expressed consistently. Given that this was the first time the refreshed brand was being applied at this scale, collaboration and iteration were essential — each round of feedback helped sharpen both the film itself and the emerging art direction guidelines.

userExperience.description: The film had to resonate across two distinct audiences: the general public seeing Agility for the first time or in a new light, and investors and partners looking for confidence in the company's vision and trajectory. The design had to feel credible and premium to both.
insights: A brand film serves as the highest-fidelity expression of a company's identity — every visual choice carries disproportionate weight
On-screen graphics need to feel native to the film's visual language, not like design elements layered on top
Investor and partner audiences require a level of polish and intentionality that goes beyond what typical marketing content demands

caseStudy.solution: The final film debuted Agility's refreshed visual identity at scale, with on-screen graphics and brand treatments that established a new visual standard for the company. The art direction and design decisions made during production became the foundation for Agility's broader art direction guidelines going forward. The film was used across external marketing channels and shared with investors and partners as a definitive statement of where Agility is headed.

images.caption: Visual brand direction applied throughout the film

results: Successfully debuted Agility's refreshed visual identity to external audiences and investors/partners
Established art direction guidelines that continue to inform the company's visual output
Served as the company's primary brand asset for both marketing and investor/partner contexts
Set a new production and design standard as Agility's first brand film at this scale
